sections.heading: Research Areas
sections.content: Dr. Tsui is a leading scholar on internet freedom, digital rights, and authoritarian information control. His research examines internet censorship, digital security, press freedom, and the dynamics of authoritarianism in the digital age — with a particular focus on China, Hong Kong, and broader Asia-Pacific contexts. He has collaborated with The Citizen Lab at the University of Toronto and brings both legal and communication perspectives to his interdisciplinary work.

sections.heading: Selected Publications
items: Over 26 journal articles and book chapters in Journalism, Chinese Journal of Communication, and Digital Journalism
Co-editor, The Hyperlinked Society (University of Michigan Press, 2008)
Research published through The Citizen Lab, University of Toronto, on internet censorship and digital security
